title: Implement Prometheus exposition format renderer from registry snapshots
purpose: Render the MetricsSnapshot from the registry into Prometheus text exposition format so that standard Prometheus scrapers can ingest liminal metrics. The renderer is a pure function from snapshot to string — it does not own an HTTP endpoint (that is the server cluster's responsibility). Rendering from a snapshot guarantees a consistent view across all metric families.
task: Create crates/liminal/src/metrics/export.rs containing the Prometheus text exposition renderer. The renderer takes a MetricsSnapshot and produces a String in Prometheus 0.0.4 text format. It must handle counters, gauges, and histograms with labels, TYPE and HELP lines, and proper escaping. The renderer is a library function — HTTP serving is out of scope.

requirements.title: Implement render function from MetricsSnapshot to Prometheus text
requirements.spec: export::render(snapshot: &MetricsSnapshot) -> String SHALL produce valid Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition format. Each metric family SHALL have a # TYPE line and a # HELP line. Counters SHALL have type counter, gauges SHALL have type gauge, histograms SHALL have type histogram. The output SHALL be a single UTF-8 string with newline-separated lines. render SHALL NOT perform any I/O.
acceptance: render(&snapshot) returns a String
Output contains # TYPE lines with correct metric types
Output contains # HELP lines for each metric family
Output ends with a newline
render is a pure function with no side effects

requirements.title: Render counter metrics
requirements.spec: Counter metrics SHALL be rendered as metric_name{labels} value where value is the counter's current u64 value. The metric name SHALL be sanitised to contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_:] characters. The # TYPE line SHALL declare the metric as counter.
acceptance: A counter named 'channel_message_rate' with label channel='orders' and value 42 renders as channel_message_rate{channel="orders"} 42
The # TYPE line reads # TYPE channel_message_rate counter
Invalid characters in metric names are replaced with underscores

requirements.title: Render gauge metrics
requirements.spec: Gauge metrics SHALL be rendered as metric_name{labels} value where value is the gauge's current i64 value. The # TYPE line SHALL declare the metric as gauge.
acceptance: A gauge named 'active_conversations' with value 7 renders as active_conversations 7
A gauge with value -3 renders correctly with the negative sign
The # TYPE line reads # TYPE active_conversations gauge

requirements.title: Render histogram metrics with buckets, sum, and count
requirements.spec: Histogram metrics SHALL be rendered with _bucket{le="bound"} lines for each bucket boundary, plus a _bucket{le="+Inf"} line, a _sum line with the sum of all observations, and a _count line with the total number of observations. The # TYPE line SHALL declare the metric as histogram.
acceptance: A histogram with buckets [0.01, 0.1, 1.0] renders _bucket lines for le=0.01, le=0.1, le=1.0, and le=+Inf
The _sum line contains the sum of all observed values
The _count line contains the total observation count
The # TYPE line reads # TYPE metric_name histogram

requirements.title: Handle label escaping in Prometheus format
requirements.spec: Label values SHALL be enclosed in double quotes. Backslashes, double quotes, and newlines within label values SHALL be escaped as \\, \", and \n respectively (Prometheus escaping rules). Label names SHALL contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_] characters. Label names SHALL NOT start with __.
acceptance: A label value containing a double quote is escaped as \"
A label value containing a backslash is escaped as \\
A label value containing a newline is escaped as \n
Label names with invalid characters are sanitised

requirements.title: Consistent snapshot rendering
requirements.spec: The render function SHALL accept a MetricsSnapshot (an owned, point-in-time copy from OBS-001 R6). Because the snapshot is already a consistent copy, the renderer does not need additional synchronisation. The renderer SHALL NOT access the live MetricsRegistry directly. This guarantees that the rendered output reflects a single consistent moment.
acceptance: render takes &MetricsSnapshot, not &MetricsRegistry
No reference to MetricsRegistry appears in export.rs
The same snapshot rendered twice produces identical output

requirements.title: Add export module to metrics mod.rs
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod export and re-export the render function.
acceptance: metrics/mod.rs contains pub mod export
The render function is re-exported from the metrics module
cargo check -p liminal passes

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement an HTTP endpoint or server — that is the server cluster's responsibility.
SHALL NOT define metric types or the registry — that is OBS-001.
SHALL NOT collect or record metrics — the renderer only reads snapshots.
SHALL NOT add a dependency on the prometheus crate — the renderer is hand-rolled.
SHALL NOT implement OpenMetrics format — only Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition.
SHALL NOT buffer or cache rendered output — each call produces a fresh render.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
render is a pure function taking &MetricsSnapshot and returning String.
Output parses as valid Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition format.
Label escaping handles backslashes, double quotes, and newlines.
The same snapshot rendered twice produces byte-identical output.
